schools.summary: Gill: Aaron is named first as the eldest, his descendants the priests; Moses' posterity remained obscure, only Levites, swallowed up among the Kohathites.
Keil & Delitzsch: the verse indicates at the outset the position the Levites were to occupy in relation to the priests.

themes: the generations of Aaron and Moses
Aaron's line the priests, Moses' the Levites
the order of God's house fixed

schools.summary: Gill, with Jarchi: the sons of Aaron are called the generations of Moses because he taught them the law — whoever teaches his neighbour's son the law, the Scripture accounts of him as if he begat him.
Keil & Delitzsch: the names of Aaron's sons, the anointed priests whose hand was filled, appointed to the priesthood (Lev. 8:12).

themes: the names of the sons of Aaron; Nadab the firstborn, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar
reckoned to Moses their teacher
teaching the law a kind of fatherhood

schools.summary: Gill: they were anointed with the holy anointing oil, with which Aaron and they only were anointed.
Keil & Delitzsch: the sons of Aaron, the priests which were anointed, consecrated to minister in the priest's office.

themes: the priests which were anointed... to minister in the priest's office
anointed with the holy oil
the priesthood a divine calling, not seized

schools.summary: Gill: they died by fire from the LORD for offering strange fire, and so were not alive when this account of the priests and Levites was taken.
Ellicott: to die childless was regarded as a reproach and a judgment, especially for Nadab and Abihu, who sinned in their office (Lev. 10:1-2).

themes: Nadab and Abihu died... when they offered strange fire
they had no children
those nearest the altar most severely judged

schools.summary: Gill: this follows the order for numbering and encamping Israel. Calvin: the passage has two heads — the Levites set apart for the ministry of the sanctuary, and bound to obey the chief priests of Aaron's family, doing nothing but by their authority; the tribe was divinely chosen, that none think himself worthy of so honourable a charge, but know it depends on the gratuitous calling of God.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses
the Levites set apart for the sanctuary under the priests
the ministry a gift of God's gratuitous calling

schools.summary: Gill: 'bring near' is to separate them for special service about the sanctuary, given a nearer place than the other tribes, and brought near to Aaron.
Keil & Delitzsch: 'stand before' marks the position of a servant standing before his master to receive his commands.

themes: Bring the tribe of Levi near... present them before Aaron
that they may minister unto him
nearness a privilege, service a subordination

schools.summary: Gill: the charge was to secure the sanctuary from being polluted or plundered, easing the priests and the people of what would otherwise be incumbent on them.
Keil & Delitzsch: they kept the charge of Aaron and the whole congregation before the tabernacle, attending to its service.

themes: they shall keep his charge, and the charge of the whole congregation
the service of the tabernacle
a trust borne for all Israel

schools.summary: Gill: they took care of the instruments and parts of the tabernacle, that none be lost or damaged, especially while it was moving and carried from place to place.
Keil & Delitzsch: they kept all the instruments of the tabernacle and the charge of the children of Israel, to do its service.

themes: they shall keep all the instruments of the tabernacle
none lost or damaged on the march
the holy things a sacred trust

schools.summary: Gill: the Levites were given to Aaron and his sons to be their ministers and servants.

Ellicott: the emphatic repetition of nethunim ('given, given') marks their being wholly given; not to be confounded with the later Nethinim, the Gibeonites made hewers of wood (Ezra 2:43); they are given to the LORD in place of the firstborn (Num. 8:16) and by him to the priests.

themes: thou shalt give the Levites unto Aaron... wholly given unto him
the emphatic 'given, given'
set apart twice over, to God and to his ministers

schools.summary: Gill: the appointment of Aaron and his sons is renewed and repeated to confirm it and charge them to execute it carefully.
Keil & Delitzsch: no stranger, no one who was not a son of Aaron, could approach the sanctuary without being put to death (cf. Num. 1:51; Lev. 22:10).

themes: thou shalt appoint Aaron and his sons... the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death
the office Aaron's alone
a solemn fence about the priesthood

schools.summary: Gill: the LORD continued to speak, giving the reason for appointing the Levites to minister to the priests and serve the tabernacle.

Keil & Delitzsch: God appointed the Levites because he had decided to adopt them as his own in place of all the firstborn, whom he sanctified to himself when he smote the firstborn of Egypt (Exod. 13:1-2).

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses
the Levites taken in place of the firstborn
the calling resting on the redemption of the Exodus

schools.summary: Gill: this was God's own act and deed, done of his sovereign will and pleasure, who may do whatsoever he pleases and whom none may contradict.
Keil & Delitzsch: the Levites taken instead of all the firstborn that openeth the womb, therefore the Levites shall be the LORD's.

themes: I, behold, I have taken the Levites... instead of all the firstborn
therefore the Levites shall be mine
the ministry of God's sovereign appointing

schools.summary: Gill: the firstborn are God's in a special way, his own, hallowed to him for the reason given.
Ellicott: the command sanctifying the firstborn, both of man and beast, is recorded just after the Exodus and the institution of the Passover (Exod. 13:1-2).

themes: all the firstborn are mine... I hallowed unto me all the firstborn in Israel
the day that I smote... Egypt
the redeemed bought out of death to be God's (1 Pet. 2:9)

schools.summary: Gill: the LORD spake at the same time and place as the foregoing declaration, the people then at Sinai.

Henry: the Levites were delivered to Aaron by tale, that he might know what he had; they were numbered by a different rule — every male from a month old, whereas the other tribes were numbered from twenty years and of those able for war.

themes: the LORD spake unto Moses in the wilderness of Sinai
the Levites numbered from a month old
a tribe wholly God's, not soldiers

schools.summary: Gill: Levi was left out of the general muster but now numbered alone, in a different manner and for a different purpose, the LORD having special regard and service for them.
Keil & Delitzsch: number the children of Levi by their families, every male from a month old and upward.

themes: every male from a month old and upward shalt thou number them
Levi counted apart
the tribe God's even from infancy

schools.summary: Gill: Moses took the account of all from a month old in the families of Levi, Aaron also concerned with him in it.
Clarke: though Moses and Aaron together numbered the twelve tribes, Moses alone numbered the Levites, since the redemption money of the surplus firstborn was to be paid to Aaron and his sons.

themes: Moses numbered them according to the word of the LORD
Moses, not Aaron, counted Levi
the count in strict obedience

schools.summary: Gill: the immediate offspring of the patriarch Levi — Gershon, Kohath, and Merari. Calvin: the tribe made an astonishing progress from a small beginning, Levi begetting but three sons, yet grown into many thousands — God working powerfully under the semblance of weakness; and he promoted the family of Kohath above the others not by worth but by his free choice.

themes: the sons of Levi by their names; Gershon, and Kohath, and Merari
a small beginning grown to thousands
Kohath promoted by God's free choice

schools.summary: Gill: records Gershon, the first son of Levi, and the families that sprang from him, the tribe reckoned by the three heads Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

themes: the sons of Gershon, by their families
the three sons of Levi
the service ordered by fathers' houses

schools.summary: Gill: records Kohath, the second son of Levi, and the families that sprang from him, the tribe reckoned by the three heads Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

themes: the sons of Kohath, by their families
the three sons of Levi
the service ordered by fathers' houses

schools.summary: Gill: records Merari, the third son of Levi, and the families that sprang from him, the tribe reckoned by the three heads Gershon, Kohath, and Merari.

themes: the sons of Merari, by their families
the three sons of Levi
the service ordered by fathers' houses

schools.summary: Gill: the two families sprung from Gershon, the first son of Levi — the Libnites and the Shimites.

themes: the family of the Libnites, and the family of the Shimites
the families of the Gershonites
the house of Levi set in array

schools.summary: Gill and Keil & Delitzsch: the Gershonites numbered seven thousand five hundred males from a month old, with their place and charge following.

themes: from a month old and upward... seven thousand and five hundred
the strength of the Gershonites known
the service carried on by number and order

schools.summary: Gill: the two families, the Libnites and Shimites, assigned their station behind the tabernacle westward.
Ellicott: the position of the priests and Levites was fixed — Moses and Aaron on the east, the Kohathites on the south, the Gershonites on the west, the Merarites on the north.

themes: The families of the Gershonites shall pitch behind the tabernacle westward
each Levite house its own quarter
the ministers stationed about God's dwelling

schools.summary: Gill: Eliasaph the son of Lael had the chief authority and direction over the Gershonites in what they were to do.

themes: the chief of the house of the father of the Gershonites shall be Eliasaph
an appointed head over each Levite house
the service ordered under leaders

schools.summary: Gill: the Libnites and Shimites were charged with the tabernacle, tent, covering, and door-hanging.
Ellicott: distinguishes the ohel (the tent, the outer covering) from the mishkan (the tabernacle, the building of boards and curtains within); the covering includes the outer coverings.

themes: the charge of the sons of Gershon... the tabernacle, and the tent, the covering... and the hanging for the door
the cloth furnishings of the sanctuary
the service exactly parcelled out

schools.summary: Gill: the hangings formed the great open court that encompassed the tabernacle, a hundred cubits long and fifty broad.
Ellicott: the cords belonged to the tabernacle itself and its furnishings, the suffix referring to each of the various things, for everything in their service.

themes: the hangings of the court, and the curtain for the door of the court
the cords of it for all the service thereof
the smallest cords numbered in the holy service

schools.summary: The families of Kohath: 'and of Kohath was the family of the Amramites, and the family of the Izeharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites: these are the families of the Kohathites' — the four families descended from Kohath, the second son of Levi, named: Amramites, Izeharites, Hebronites, Uzzielites (the the families of the Kohathites, the four houses of Kohath, the figure, the families of Kohath recorded, the house from which Aaron and Moses came, Exodus 6:18, the most honored of the Levitical houses, charged with the holiest things, the ordered recording of the families, each house of Levi set down); the families of Kohath (the houses descended from him, Exodus 6:18) — the families (the houses of Kohath); 'these are the families of the Kohathites' (the four families of Kohath, the house of Aaron and Moses, Exodus 6:18, the figure, the honored house of Levi, the providence, the families recorded, their charge to follow, vv.28-32) — Wesley, Gill.

themes: And of Kohath was the family of the Amramites, and the family of the Izeharites, and the family of the Hebronites, and the family of the Uzzielites: these are the families of the Kohathites
the families of Kohath
the four families of Kohath, the house from which Aaron and Moses came (Exodus 6:18)
the honored Levitical house, each set down in the record

schools.summary: The number of the Kohathites and their charge of the sanctuary: 'in the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were eight thousand and six hundred, keeping the charge of the sanctuary' — the males of Kohath from a month old were 8,600, charged with keeping the sanctuary (the from a month old and upward, the Levites numbered from infancy, not from twenty as the men of war, for they were the LORD's, vv.15, the eight thousand and six hundred, the number of the Kohathites, the keeping the charge of the sanctuary, their office, the care of the holy things, the figure, the Kohathites charged with the holiest things, the ark and the vessels, Numbers 4:4-15, the most honored charge, the care of the sanctuary's holy furniture, the Levites set to the keeping of the holy things, the service of God); the Kohathites numbered from a month old (the Levites counted from infancy, vv.15) — the count (the Kohathites numbered); 'keeping the charge of the sanctuary' (the care of the holy things, the Kohathites' office, Numbers 4:4-15, the figure, the keeping of the holy things, the honored charge, the providence, the Kohathites' charge, their place to follow, vv.29-31) — JFB, Gill.

themes: In the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were eight thousand and six hundred, keeping the charge of the sanctuary
the number of the Kohathites and their charge of the sanctuary
the Kohathites numbered from a month old, charged with the holiest things (vv.15)
the keeping of the sanctuary's holy furniture (Numbers 4:4-15)

schools.summary: The place of the Kohathites on the south side: 'the families of the sons of Kohath shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward' — the Kohathites encamped on the south side of the tabernacle (the pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward, their assigned place in the camp, the figure, the ordered camp of the Levites around the tabernacle, each family in its place, the Kohathites on the south, the order of God's house, every family assigned its station, the Levites surrounding the tabernacle to guard it, the place each had in the service, the order that prevents confusion, 'let all things be done decently and in order,' 1 Corinthians 14:40); the Kohathite families (the houses of Kohath) — the families (the Kohathites); 'shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward' (the south side assigned, the ordered camp, 1 Corinthians 14:40, the figure, each family in its place, the order of God's house, the providence, the Kohathites' place, their chief to follow, vv.30) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: The families of the sons of Kohath shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle southward
the place of the Kohathites on the south side
the Kohathites in their assigned station, each Levitical family ordered around the tabernacle
the order of God's house (1 Corinthians 14:40)

schools.summary: The chief of the Kohathites: 'and the chief of the house of the father of the families of the Kohathites shall be Elizaphan the son of Uzziel' — the chief over the Kohathite families was Elizaphan the son of Uzziel (the the chief of the house of the father, the head of the Kohathite families, the Elizaphan the son of Uzziel, the named chief, the figure, the head of the Kohathites recorded by name, the leader of the honored house, the order that sets a chief over each family, the recognized head, Elizaphan named, of the family of Uzziel, the youngest of Kohath's sons, Exodus 6:18, 22); the head of the Kohathite families (the chief) — the chief (the leader of Kohath); 'shall be Elizaphan the son of Uzziel' (the named chief, Elizaphan, of Uzziel, Exodus 6:22, the figure, the head of the house recorded, the providence, the chief named, the charge to follow, vv.31) — Gill.

themes: And the chief of the house of the father of the families of the Kohathites shall be Elizaphan the son of Uzziel
the chief of the Kohathites
the chief over the Kohathite families, the head of the honored house recorded by name (Exodus 6:22)
the order that sets a leader over each family

schools.summary: The charge of the Kohathites, the holiest things: 'and their charge shall be the ark, and the table, and the candlestick, and the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary wherewith they minister, and the hanging, and all the service thereof' — the Kohathites charged with the holiest furniture: the ark, the table, the candlestick, the altars, the vessels, and the veil (the the ark, the most holy, the throne of God, the the table... candlestick... altars... vessels... hanging, the holy furniture, the all the service thereof, their care of these things, the figure, the Kohathites charged with the holiest things, the ark and the furniture of the sanctuary, the most honored service, the care of the things that figured Christ — the ark, the mercy seat, the table, the bread, the candlestick, the light, the altars, the sacrifice and incense, the holy charge of bearing and tending the sacred things, the highest Levitical service); the Kohathites' charge of the holiest furniture (the ark and the rest) — the charge (the holy things); 'the vessels of the sanctuary wherewith they minister, and the hanging, and all the service thereof' (the vessels and the veil, the holy things tended, the figure, the things that figured Christ, the holy charge, the providence, the Kohathites' charge, the Merarites to follow, vv.33-37) — Wesley, Gill.

themes: And their charge shall be the ark, and the table, and the candlestick, and the altars, and the vessels of the sanctuary wherewith they minister, and the hanging, and all the service thereof
the charge of the Kohathites: the holiest things
the Kohathites charged with the holy furniture, the ark and the vessels that figured Christ
the highest Levitical service

schools.summary: Eleazar as chief over the Levites: 'and Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall be chief over the chief of the Levites, and have the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary' — Eleazar, Aaron's son, set as chief over all the Levite chiefs, overseeing those who kept the sanctuary (the chief over the chief of the Levites, the head over all the Levitical heads, the highest under Aaron, the the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary, the supervision of the sanctuary's keepers, the figure, Eleazar the chief overseer, the priest set over the Levites, the order of authority, the priest over the Levites, the under-shepherds set over the service, Eleazar, who would succeed Aaron, the chief of the Levitical oversight, Numbers 20:28); Eleazar set as chief over the Levite heads (the highest oversight) — the chief (the head over all); 'have the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary' (the supervision of the sanctuary's keepers, Eleazar's office, Numbers 20:28, the figure, the priest over the Levites, the order of authority, the providence, Eleazar's oversight, the Merarites to follow, vv.33) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest shall be chief over the chief of the Levites, and have the oversight of them that keep the charge of the sanctuary
Eleazar as chief over the Levites
Eleazar the priest set over all the Levite heads, the supervision of the sanctuary's keepers
the order of authority under Aaron (Numbers 20:28)

schools.summary: The families of Merari: 'of Merari was the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari' — the two families descended from Merari, the third son of Levi, were the Mahlites and the Mushites (the the families of Merari, the two houses of Merari, Mahli and Mushi, Exodus 6:19, the figure, the families of Merari recorded, the third Levitical house, the ordered recording of all the houses of Levi, each family set down, Merari charged with the heavier parts of the tabernacle, the boards and bars, vv.36-37); the families of Merari (the houses descended from him, Exodus 6:19) — the families (the houses of Merari); 'the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari' (the two families of Merari, Mahli and Mushi, Exodus 6:19, the figure, the third house of Levi recorded, the providence, the families recorded, their number and charge to follow, vv.34-37) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: Of Merari was the family of the Mahlites, and the family of the Mushites: these are the families of Merari
the families of Merari
the two families of Merari, the third house of Levi (Exodus 6:19)
each set down in the record

schools.summary: The number of the Merarites: 'and those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were six thousand and two hundred' — the males of Merari from a month old were 6,200 (the from a month old and upward, the Levites numbered from infancy, vv.15, the six thousand and two hundred, the number of the Merarites, the figure, the Merarites numbered, the third Levitical house counted, the precise reckoning of each Levite family, the houses of Levi set down by number, the ordered count of the servants of God's house, each family numbered); the Merarites numbered from a month old (vv.15) — the count (the Merarites numbered); 'were six thousand and two hundred' (the number of the Merarites, 6,200, the figure, the family counted, the providence, the Merarites numbered, their place to follow, vv.35) — JFB, Gill.

themes: And those that were numbered of them, according to the number of all the males, from a month old and upward, were six thousand and two hundred
the number of the Merarites
the Merarites numbered from a month old, the third Levitical house counted (vv.15)
the ordered reckoning of the servants of God's house

schools.summary: The chief and the northward place of the Merarites: 'and the chief of the house of the father of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail: these shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward' — the chief of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail, and the Merarites encamped on the north side (the the chief... was Zuriel the son of Abihail, the named head of Merari, the these shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward, their assigned place, the north, the figure, the head of Merari recorded and the family's place assigned, the ordered camp, each Levitical family in its station around the tabernacle, the Merarites on the north, the order of God's house, every family with its chief and its place); the head of Merari named (Zuriel) — the chief (the leader of Merari); 'these shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward' (the north side assigned, the ordered camp, the figure, each family in its place, the providence, the Merarites' place, their charge to follow, vv.36-37) — Gill.

themes: And the chief of the house of the father of the families of Merari was Zuriel the son of Abihail: these shall pitch on the side of the tabernacle northward
the chief and the northward place of the Merarites
the head of Merari recorded by name and the family's place assigned on the north
each Levitical family with its chief and station

schools.summary: The charge of the Merarites, the framework of the tabernacle: 'and under the custody and charge of the sons of Merari shall be the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars thereof, and the pillars thereof, and the sockets thereof, and all the vessels thereof, and all that serveth thereto' — the Merarites charged with the heavy framework: the boards, bars, pillars, sockets, and all that pertained (the the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars... pillars... sockets, the structural framework, the heavy parts, the all the vessels thereof, and all that serveth thereto, the fittings, the figure, the Merarites charged with the framework, the boards and bars that held the tabernacle up, the less honored but necessary service, the heavy burden-bearing, the figure that every part of God's house has its keepers, the structure as well as the holy things, each Levite with his service, the body fitly framed together, Ephesians 2:21); the Merarites' charge of the boards and bars (the heavy framework) — the charge (the structure); 'the pillars thereof, and the sockets thereof, and all the vessels thereof, and all that serveth thereto' (the pillars, sockets, and fittings, the structural parts, the figure, every part of God's house has its keepers, Ephesians 2:21, the providence, the Merarites' charge, the priests' place to follow, vv.38) — Gill.

themes: And under the custody and charge of the sons of Merari shall be the boards of the tabernacle, and the bars thereof, and the pillars thereof, and the sockets thereof, and all the vessels thereof, and all that serveth thereto
the charge of the Merarites: the framework of the tabernacle
the Merarites charged with the structure, the boards and bars and pillars, the necessary burden-bearing
every part of God's house with its keepers (Ephesians 2:21)

schools.summary: The Merarites' charge of the court's pillars and cords: 'and the pillars of the court round about, and their sockets, and their pins, and their cords' — the Merarites also charged with the pillars of the surrounding court, their sockets, pins, and cords (the the pillars of the court round about, the posts of the outer court, the their sockets, and their pins, and their cords, the fittings that held the court, the figure, the Merarites charged even with the smallest parts, the pins and cords, the care of every part, even the least, the truth that nothing in God's house is too small to be tended, every pin and cord has its keeper, the humble but necessary service, the whole framework held together by the least parts); the Merarites' charge of the court's pillars (the outer court) — the charge (the court's structure); 'their pins, and their cords' (even the pins and cords charged, the smallest parts, the figure, nothing too small to be tended, the providence, the Merarites' full charge, the priests' place to follow, vv.38) — Ellicott, Gill.

themes: And the pillars of the court round about, and their sockets, and their pins, and their cords
the Merarites' charge of the court's pillars and cords
the Merarites charged even with the smallest parts, the pins and cords
nothing in God's house too small to be tended, every part with its keeper

schools.summary: The priests' charge on the east and the stranger put to death: 'but those that encamp before the tabernacle toward the east, even before the tabernacle of the congregation eastward, shall be Moses, and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the charge of the children of Israel; and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death' — the charge — 'Moses, and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary... and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death': Moses and the priests encamped on the east, the place of honor before the entrance, keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the people, and any unauthorized person (stranger) who came near to die (the witnesses noting the significance — the priests in the place of honor, the east, before the entrance, the most honored station, keeping the charge of the sanctuary on behalf of all Israel, the lesson — the holiness that guards the sanctuary, the priests as the appointed keepers, and the solemn law that the stranger — any not of the priesthood — who came near the holy things should die, the holiness that none may approach but those God appoints, the warning against intrusion into the holy office, as Korah and his company would learn, Numbers 16:40, and Uzzah, 2 Samuel 6:7, and Uzziah the king, 2 Chronicles 26:18-21, the figure — the holiness of God that admits only the appointed near, the priesthood that guards the way, a figure of Christ the one Mediator through whom alone we draw near, 'no man cometh unto the Father, but by me,' John 14:6, the boldness to enter only by his blood, Hebrews 10:19-22, the holiness that both guards the sanctuary and points to the one appointed way of access); the priests on the east keeping the charge of the sanctuary for the people (the place of honor, the appointed keepers) — the charge (the priests as keepers); 'the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death' (the stranger, the unauthorized, put to death for coming near, the holiness guarded, Numbers 16:40, the figure, only the appointed may approach, John 14:6, the providence, the sanctuary guarded, the access through Christ) — Calvin, Ellicott, Wesley, Gill.

debates.question: What does the priests' charge and the stranger's death teach?

sides.position: The appointed keepers in the place of honor, guarding the sanctuary, and the solemn law that none unauthorized may approach the holy things (Numbers 16:40; 2 Chronicles 26:18-21) - a figure of the holiness that admits only the appointed near and of Christ the one Mediator through whom alone we draw near (John 14:6; Hebrews 10:19-22)

themes: But those that encamp before the tabernacle toward the east... shall be Moses, and Aaron and his sons, keeping the charge of the sanctuary... and the stranger that cometh nigh shall be put to death
the priests' charge on the east and the stranger put to death (the crux of the holiness that guards the sanctuary)
the holiness that guards the sanctuary, admitting only the appointed near (Numbers 16:40; 2 Chronicles 26:18-21)
a figure of Christ the one Mediator through whom alone we draw near (John 14:6; Hebrews 10:19-22)

schools.summary: The total number of the Levites: 'all that were numbered of the Levites, which Moses and Aaron numbered at the commandment of the LORD, throughout their families, all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty and two thousand' — the total of the Levites, all males from a month old, was 22,000 (the all that were numbered of the Levites, the sum of the three families, Gershon, Kohath, Merari, the from a month old and upward, the Levites counted from infancy, vv.15, the twenty and two thousand, the total, the round number, though the three families summed slightly more, the difference noted by interpreters, the 300 firstborn Levites perhaps not counted, the figure, the Levites numbered as a whole, the tribe set apart to God, the count that would be matched against the firstborn of Israel, vv.40-43, the Levites the LORD's, taken instead of the firstborn); the total of the Levites numbered (the three families summed, vv.15) — the sum (the Levites' total); 'all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty and two thousand' (the total 22,000, the round number, the firstborn comparison to follow, vv.40-43, the figure, the Levites the LORD's, the providence, the Levite total, the firstborn numbered next, vv.40) — Keil and Delitzsch, Gill.

themes: All that were numbered of the Levites... all the males from a month old and upward, were twenty and two thousand
the total number of the Levites
the Levites numbered as a whole, the tribe set apart to God (vv.15)
the count to be matched against the firstborn of Israel

schools.summary: The command to number the firstborn of Israel: 'and the LORD said unto Moses, Number all the firstborn of the males of the children of Israel from a month old and upward, and take the number of their names' — God commanding Moses to number the firstborn males of Israel from a month old, to be matched against the Levites (the Number all the firstborn of the males, the firstborn counted, the count to match the Levites, the from a month old and upward, as the Levites were counted, vv.15, the take the number of their names, each firstborn named and counted, the figure, the firstborn numbered to be exchanged for the Levites, the basis of the substitution, the firstborn that belonged to God by the Passover, Exodus 13:2, 15, now to be redeemed by the setting apart of the Levites, the count that prepares the great exchange, vv.41-51); the firstborn counted from a month old (to match the Levites, Exodus 13:2) — the count (the firstborn numbered); 'take the number of their names' (each firstborn named and counted, the basis of the exchange, Exodus 13:15, the figure, the firstborn that belonged to God, to be redeemed, the providence, the firstborn numbered, the Levites taken instead, vv.41) — Henry, JFB, Keil, Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites taken instead of the firstborn: 'and thou shalt take the Levites for me (I am the LORD) instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel; and the cattle of the Levites instead of all the firstlings among the cattle of the children of Israel' — the substitution — 'thou shalt take the Levites for me... instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel': God took the Levites for himself in place of all the firstborn of Israel, and the Levites' cattle in place of the firstlings (the witnesses noting the significance — the substitution of the Levites for the firstborn, the firstborn belonged to God by right, having been spared in the Passover when the firstborn of Egypt died, Exodus 13:2, 15, now God took the whole tribe of Levi in their stead, that the firstborn might be redeemed and the Levites devoted to his service, the lesson — the principle of substitution and redemption, one taken in the place of another, the Levites standing in for the firstborn, the tribe given to God instead of the firstborn of every house, the figure — the great gospel principle of substitution, one standing in the place of others, fulfilled in Christ who is taken in our stead, 'the firstborn among many brethren,' Romans 8:29, who redeems his people, the firstborn of God by adoption, 'the church of the firstborn, which are written in heaven,' Hebrews 12:23, the Levites a figure of the redemption that takes one for another, and Christ the true substitute who stands for his people, the I am the LORD sealing the divine right and the divine claim, the figure of the substitute taken for the redeemed); the Levites taken instead of the firstborn (the substitution, the firstborn God's by the Passover, Exodus 13:15) — the exchange (one for another); 'the cattle of the Levites instead of all the firstlings among the cattle' (the Levites' cattle taken for the firstlings, the exchange extended to the beasts) — the figure (the full substitution, Romans 8:29, the providence, the Levites the LORD's, the surplus firstborn to be redeemed, vv.46-48) — Calvin, Keil, Wesley, Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the taking of the Levites instead of the firstborn signify?

sides.position: The firstborn that belonged to God by the Passover redeemed by the setting apart of the Levites (Exodus 13:15), the principle of one taken in the place of another - a figure of the gospel substitution fulfilled in Christ, the firstborn among many brethren who stands in our stead and redeems his people (Romans 8:29; Hebrews 12:23)

schools.summary: Moses numbering the firstborn as commanded: 'and Moses numbered, as the LORD commanded him, all the firstborn among the children of Israel' — Moses obeyed and numbered all the firstborn of Israel (the Moses numbered, the obedience, the count performed, the as the LORD commanded him, the exact obedience, vv.40, the all the firstborn among the children of Israel, the whole number of the firstborn, the figure, the obedience of Moses in numbering the firstborn, the count done as commanded, the firstborn numbered for the exchange with the Levites, the faithful doing of God's word, 'as the LORD commanded,' the refrain of Moses's obedience); Moses numbering as commanded (the exact obedience, vv.40) — the obedience (the count performed); 'all the firstborn among the children of Israel' (the whole number of the firstborn, counted for the exchange, the figure, the firstborn numbered, the providence, the count done, the number to follow, vv.43) — Gill.

schools.summary: The number of the firstborn, 22,273: 'and all the firstborn males by the number of names, from a month old and upward, of those that were numbered of them, were twenty and two thousand two hundred and threescore and thirteen' — the firstborn of Israel numbered 22,273, exceeding the Levites (22,000) by 273 (the all the firstborn males by the number of names, the firstborn counted by name, the twenty and two thousand two hundred and threescore and thirteen, 22,273, the number of the firstborn, the figure, the firstborn 273 more than the Levites, vv.39, the surplus that must be redeemed with money, vv.46-48, the exact count that reveals the excess, the 273 for whom no Levite could be exchanged, to be redeemed by the redemption price, the careful reckoning that leaves none unaccounted, every firstborn provided for); the firstborn numbered by name (the count) — the count (the firstborn numbered); 'twenty and two thousand two hundred and threescore and thirteen' (the 22,273, exceeding the Levites by 273, the surplus to be redeemed, vv.46-48, the figure, the excess provided for by the redemption price, the providence, the surplus firstborn, the redemption to follow, vv.44-48) — Clarke, Gill.

schools.summary: The LORD speaking concerning the redemption of the surplus firstborn: 'and the LORD spake unto Moses, saying' — the LORD spoke to Moses again, now to direct the redemption of the 273 firstborn beyond the number of the Levites (the speaking, God's command, the new direction, the occasion, after the count revealed the surplus of 273, vv.43, now the provision for their redemption, the figure, God ordering the redemption of the surplus, that none be unaccounted, the divine direction for the exact and just exchange, the surplus firstborn provided for by the redemption price, God directing even the redemption of the surplus, showing his care that all be justly settled, none left without redemption); the LORD speaking to Moses (the redemption of the surplus) — the new direction (the redemption price); the LORD spake unto Moses — the redemption of the surplus introduced (the providence, God ordering the just exchange, vv.45-48) — Gill.

schools.summary: The Levites taken and made the LORD's: 'take the Levites instead of all the firstborn among the children of Israel, and the cattle of the Levites instead of their cattle; and the Levites shall be mine: I am the LORD' — the command repeated: the Levites taken instead of the firstborn, their cattle for the firstlings, and the Levites made the LORD's own (the take the Levites instead of all the firstborn, the substitution confirmed, vv.41, the the Levites shall be mine, the Levites God's possession, devoted to him, the I am the LORD, the seal of the divine claim, the figure, the Levites made the LORD's by the exchange, the tribe wholly devoted to God, the truth of belonging to God, the redeemed made his own, 'ye are not your own... ye are bought with a price,' 1 Corinthians 6:19-20, the Levites a figure of those who are God's possession, taken for himself, the LORD's own people); the Levites and their cattle taken for the firstborn and firstlings (the substitution, vv.41) — the exchange (the Levites for the firstborn); 'the Levites shall be mine: I am the LORD' (the Levites God's own, devoted to him, 1 Corinthians 6:19-20, the figure, the redeemed made God's possession, the providence, the Levites the LORD's, the surplus to be redeemed, vv.46-48) — Calvin, Gill.

schools.summary: The redemption price of five shekels: 'thou shalt even take five shekels apiece by the poll, after the shekel of the sanctuary shalt thou take them: (the shekel is twenty gerahs:)' — the price — 'thou shalt even take five shekels apiece by the poll, after the shekel of the sanctuary': each of the 273 surplus firstborn redeemed with five shekels of the sanctuary, twenty gerahs to the shekel (the witnesses noting the significance — the redemption price, five shekels a head, the fixed sum of redemption, by the standard weight of the sanctuary, the lesson — the principle of redemption by a price, the firstborn beyond the exchange could not be covered by a Levite, so were redeemed with money, the redemption price paid for each soul, the same five shekels as the redemption of the firstborn elsewhere, Numbers 18:16, the figure — the redemption of souls by a price, fulfilled in Christ who redeemed us not with silver but with his own blood, 'ye were not redeemed with corruptible things, as silver and gold... but with the precious blood of Christ,' 1 Peter 1:18-19, 'ye are bought with a price,' 1 Corinthians 6:20, 'in whom we have redemption through his blood,' Ephesians 1:7, the shekel of the sanctuary marking the divine standard, the exact and appointed price, the figure of the precious redemption, the price paid for each soul, a shadow of the infinite price of Christ's blood, the five shekels a token of the redemption that Christ would accomplish in full); the five shekels a head (the redemption sum, Numbers 18:16) — the price (the redemption money); the shekel of the sanctuary, twenty gerahs (the divine standard weight, Exodus 30:13) — the figure (the appointed, exact price, a shadow of Christ's redemption, 1 Peter 1:18-19, the providence, the redemption price, the money to Aaron, vv.48-51) — Ellicott, Gill.

debates.question: What does the redemption price of five shekels signify?

sides.position: The fixed redemption sum for each firstborn beyond the exchange (Numbers 18:16), by the divine standard of the sanctuary shekel - a figure of the redemption of souls by a price, fulfilled in Christ who redeemed us with his own blood (1 Peter 1:18-19; 1 Corinthians 6:20; Ephesians 1:7), the five shekels a token of the infinite price of Christ's redemption

schools.summary: The redemption money given to Aaron and his sons: 'and thou shalt give the money, wherewith the odd number of them is to be redeemed, unto Aaron and to his sons' — the redemption money for the surplus firstborn given to Aaron and his sons, the priests (the the money, wherewith the odd number of them is to be redeemed, the redemption price for the 273, the unto Aaron and to his sons, given to the priests, the figure, the redemption money given to the priesthood, the price paid into the hands of the priests, the redemption received by those who minister at the altar, the truth that the price of redemption belongs to God and his service, the money given to the priests as God's representatives, the redemption accomplished and the price received); the redemption money for the 273 (the price) — the money (the redemption price); 'unto Aaron and to his sons' (the money given to the priests, the redemption received, the figure, the price into the hands of the priesthood, the providence, the money given, the surplus redeemed, vv.49-51) — Ellicott, Gill.
